_meta.title: drone_indicator_library
_meta.description: Library of customer-facing text for every indicator at every band of the drone chain. Layered annotation ON TOP OF the canonical chain definition (drone_confidence_score_v2_1_LOCKED.json). References chain indicator IDs (L3I_IMG_*, L3I_GNSS_*, L3I_FC_*, L3I_CAL_*). INTRODUCES indicator-level null pattern (Q-DRONE-4): L3I_FC_005 wind_condition has a null band for API failure cases.

three_tier_architecture.tier_2_library_this_file: drone_indicator_library_v2_1.json — customer-voice text per indicator per band

_meta.text_authorship_status: CLAUDE-DRAFTED PLACEHOLDER. Per pattern Q1: structure locked, library text marked as placeholder pending domain refinement.

new_pattern_concepts.indicator_level_null: L3I_FC_005 wind_condition supports null band for API failure. Distinct from GCP's chain-level null. Library schema extends to allow null bands with score_range: [null, null].
new_pattern_concepts.multiple_chain_level_hard_gates: Three critical-path indicators (L3I_IMG_001, L3I_GNSS_001, L3I_FC_001). All three independently fire hard gates. global_gate_condition lists with OR.

schema_notes.null_band: Bands with score_range: [null, null] and level: "null" indicate the indicator cannot be measured. Rendering layer should surface as informational/audit-only, NOT score-affecting.
schema_notes.gate_scope: chain_level (kills entire chain) / per_point_only (degrades point in per-point chains) / none.

L3I_IMG_001.name: Image validity
L3I_IMG_001.verified_statement: All captured images are valid and usable for processing.

bands.label: 85-95% valid — significant image losses
bands.impact: A meaningful fraction of captured images are corrupted or unusable. Coverage may be reduced; processing may need to compensate with reduced overlap margin.
actions: Verify which images failed and why (storage, sensor, transmission)
Check whether failed images create coverage gaps
Inspect camera firmware and storage card health for next flight

bands.label: <85% valid (HARD GATE)
bands.impact: Too many images failed for the survey to be salvageable. Coverage gaps will be unrecoverable; the flight must be re-flown.
actions: Refly the survey with verified camera and storage hardware
Inspect camera/storage system for systemic faults before refly
Verify firmware versions match drone and camera spec

L3I_IMG_001.derivation: Hard gate at 30 reflects industry threshold for unrecoverable image loss (>15% lost). Below this, coverage cannot be maintained even with aggressive overlap.

L3I_IMG_002.name: Image geotag
L3I_IMG_002.verified_statement: Every image carries a valid GPS geotag from the rover.

bands.label: All images geotagged with rover-derived positions
Some images missing geotags or using onboard GPS only
bands.impact: Images without rover-derived geotags rely on the drone's autonomous GPS — less accurate. PPK correction skipped for those frames; positioning weaker.
actions: Verify rover-to-camera time sync was active during flight
Check log for events that disabled geotag injection
Confirm next flight maintains continuous rover-camera link

bands.label: Majority of images missing geotags
bands.impact: Most images lack rover-derived position. PPK correction provides little uplift; output accuracy may be similar to autonomous GPS (~3-5m).
actions: Inspect rover-camera sync configuration
Review time-sync calibration procedure
Consider whether this dataset meets accuracy requirements before processing

L3I_IMG_002.derivation: Geotag injection from rover is the basis for PPK position uplift. Coverage threshold 85 reflects minimum for PPK to add meaningful accuracy.

L3I_IMG_003.name: Image overlap
L3I_IMG_003.verified_statement: Captured imagery has sufficient forward and side overlap for reconstruction.

bands.label: Overlap below planned but within reconstructable range
bands.impact: Reduced overlap means weaker tie-point density. Reconstruction may have holes in low-texture areas (water, sand, snow, asphalt).
actions: Inspect orthomosaic for holes after processing
Increase planned overlap for similar future surveys
Review whether wind or speed affected actual capture density

bands.label: Overlap below reconstructable range
bands.impact: Overlap too low to reconstruct reliably. Output orthomosaic and DSM may have significant holes or distortions.
actions: Check whether speed during flight was higher than planned
Consider re-flying critical areas with tighter overlap
Verify mission planner overlap settings for next flight

L3I_IMG_003.derivation: 75/65 forward/side overlap from industry photogrammetric standards (Agisoft, Pix4D documentation). Below 60/45 reconstruction quality degrades sharply.

L3I_IMG_004.name: Image format
L3I_IMG_004.verified_statement: All images are in a consistent supported format.

bands.label: Single supported format (JPEG/TIFF/RAW), consistent across all images
Mixed formats or unsupported variants present
bands.impact: Format inconsistency can disrupt processing pipelines that expect uniform input. Mixed JPEG/RAW can cause exposure inconsistencies in the orthomosaic.
actions: Verify camera was set to single output format for the survey
If mixed, consider whether to process formats separately
Standardize camera config for future surveys

L3I_IMG_004.derivation: Format mixing is rare with modern cameras; flag is a hygiene check rather than a quality concern.

L3I_IMG_005.name: Exposure consistency
L3I_IMG_005.verified_statement: Image exposure stays consistent across the flight.

bands.label: Exposure variance within camera auto-exposure tolerance
High exposure variation across flight (hygiene signal)
bands.impact: Cloud cover or terrain shadows during flight caused exposure variations. Orthomosaic may show patches of brighter/darker areas. Reconstruction still works.
actions: Inspect ortho for visible exposure seams
Consider flying under more uniform lighting next time
Apply post-process color matching if needed

L3I_IMG_005.derivation: Q-DRONE-5 alignment: exposure variation is a hygiene signal, not a quality killer. Downgraded to minor (audit-only).

L3I_GNSS_001.name: Rover coverage
L3I_GNSS_001.verified_statement: Rover recorded continuously through the entire flight with adequate buffer.

bands.label: Rover covered full flight + ≥60s buffer either side
Rover covered flight but buffer is short or partial
bands.impact: Rover started recording too close to takeoff or stopped too soon after landing. PPK convergence may be incomplete at flight start; positions near edges may be weaker.
actions: Start rover 2-3 minutes before takeoff next time
Stop rover only after motors off (60s post-landing)
Inspect early/late epoch sigma in processed output

bands.label: Rover did not cover flight window (HARD GATE)
bands.impact: Rover RINEX has a gap during the flight. PPK cannot correct those flight epochs; positioning falls back to autonomous GPS (~3-5m accuracy).
actions: Refly with rover recording verified before takeoff
Inspect rover hardware (battery, storage, antenna connection)
Set up rover recording 2-3 minutes before mission planned start

L3I_GNSS_001.derivation:
Q-DRONE-2 promotion to chain-level hard gate. Without rover observations during flight, PPK has no reference data — output is autonomous-GPS accuracy regardless of how clean everything else is.

v2.1.1 boundary patch: resurvey band widened from [0,0] to [0,39] so pipeline scores of 20 (emitted by the threshold ladder when the bottom band fires) land in the resurvey level alongside gate-forced 0. Pipeline spec sheet 05 emits 20 in the worst band by design; the library originally assumed gates were the only path to resurvey.

L3I_GNSS_002.name: Rover signal quality
L3I_GNSS_002.verified_statement: Rover maintained strong signal-to-noise throughout the flight.

bands.label: C/N0 consistently high (≥40 dB-Hz on most satellites)
Moderate C/N0 — signal partially degraded
bands.impact: Rover signal degraded during portions of the flight. Affected epochs may have higher position uncertainty even after PPK.
actions: Check rover antenna mounting — direct line of sight to sky?
Inspect for interference sources (radio, power lines, drone radio at low altitude)
Verify rover antenna is the right band for receiver

bands.label: Poor C/N0 — significant signal issues
bands.impact: Rover signal was severely compromised. PPK output likely has elevated position uncertainty; some epochs may not converge.
actions: Inspect environment for interference (high-voltage lines, radio masts)
Replace rover antenna or cable if recurring
Choose less-obstructed setup location for next flight

L3I_GNSS_002.derivation: C/N0 thresholds from GNSS receiver documentation. 40 dB-Hz is typical 'strong signal' baseline for survey-grade receivers.

L3I_GNSS_003.name: Rover frequency
L3I_GNSS_003.verified_statement: Rover recorded at sufficient frequency (e.g., 5Hz) to support fast-moving drone.

bands.label: Recording rate below 5Hz (drone moves faster than rover samples)
bands.impact: Rover sampled too slowly for drone speed. Image geotag interpolation introduces position error, especially at flight turns.
actions: Set rover to ≥5Hz recording for next flight
Verify rover receiver capability and configuration
Consider whether this dataset meets accuracy requirements

L3I_GNSS_003.derivation: 5Hz is industry standard for survey drones flying ≤15 m/s. Higher speeds require proportionally higher rover rates.

L3I_GNSS_004.name: Rover continuity
L3I_GNSS_004.verified_statement: Rover observations are continuous with minimal cycle slips.

bands.label: Cycle slips or gaps >5s detected
bands.impact: Rover lost lock briefly during flight. PPK must re-converge after each event; position accuracy reduced in those windows.
actions: Inspect rover environment for signal blockage during flight
Check rover antenna stability (vibration, wind shaking)
Position next setup with clearer sky view

L3I_GNSS_004.derivation: Cycle slip tolerance threshold from PPK processing best practice.

L3I_GNSS_005.name: Rover acquisition
L3I_GNSS_005.verified_statement: Rover acquired satellites within normal startup time.

bands.label: >3 min — slow acquisition (hygiene signal)
bands.impact: Rover was slow to lock satellites. Usually cold start. Recurring slowness can indicate receiver health issues but rarely affects this flight's data.
actions: Check rover antenna mounting and view of sky
Verify firmware and battery state
Service receiver if slow acquisition recurs

L3I_GNSS_005.derivation: Following base_station Q4 / GCP Q-GCP pattern: acquisition slowness is hygiene signal, not deliverable-quality.

L3I_GNSS_006.name: Rover PDOP
L3I_GNSS_006.verified_statement: Strong satellite geometry during flight — PDOP within standard ranges.

bands.label: PDOP >4 during flight
bands.impact: Satellite geometry was suboptimal during flight. PPK still works but position uncertainty is elevated; sigma values higher than nominal.
actions: Use mission planner with satellite forecast for future flights
Reschedule flight if PDOP forecast >6 for accuracy-critical work
Check processed sigma values for elevated uncertainty

L3I_GNSS_006.derivation: Standard PDOP categorization from GNSS handbooks.

L3I_FC_001.name: Mission coverage
L3I_FC_001.verified_statement: Drone flew the full planned mission area.

bands.label: 95-99% covered — minor gap
bands.impact: Small portion of the planned area was not flown. Output deliverable will have a corresponding gap; check whether the missed area was operationally important.
actions: Identify which polygon edge was missed and why
Re-fly missing area if critical
Verify mission was correctly uploaded next time

bands.label: <95% of planned area covered (HARD GATE)
bands.impact: Significant portion of the planned area was not surveyed. Deliverable is fundamentally incomplete; re-fly required.
actions: Refly with mission verified on drone before takeoff
Check whether early battery, weather, or operator abort caused short flight
Allocate adequate battery margin for similar future missions

L3I_FC_001.derivation:
Q-DRONE-2 promotion to chain-level hard gate. <95% coverage means deliverable is fundamentally incomplete regardless of other quality.

v2.1.1 boundary patch: resurvey band widened from [0,0] to [0,39] so pipeline scores of 20 (emitted by the threshold ladder when the bottom band fires) land in the resurvey level alongside gate-forced 0. Pipeline spec sheet 05 emits 20 in the worst band by design; the library originally assumed gates were the only path to resurvey.

L3I_FC_002.name: GSD consistency
L3I_FC_002.verified_statement: Ground Sample Distance (GSD) is consistent with mission spec.

bands.label: GSD deviates from planned by more than 10%
bands.impact: Actual ground resolution differs from planned. Output may not meet specified resolution for client deliverable.
actions: Inspect altitude logs — was drone too high or too low?
Verify mission altitude was correctly entered
Recalculate deliverable resolution claim against actual GSD

L3I_FC_002.derivation: ±10% tolerance from photogrammetric practice — wider variance affects output GSD claims.

L3I_FC_003.name: Actual overlap
L3I_FC_003.verified_statement: Actual flown overlap matches mission specification.

bands.label: Actual overlap deviates from planned by more than 5%
bands.impact: Speed during flight differed from plan, changing capture spacing. Reconstruction may be weaker in affected regions.
actions: Check whether wind affected actual ground speed
Inspect ortho/DSM for weak-reconstruction patches
Tighten planned overlap to account for typical wind

L3I_FC_003.derivation: Couples L3I_IMG_003 image overlap — same concern measured from different sources (image inter-distance vs flight log).

L3I_FC_004.name: Flight altitude consistency
L3I_FC_004.verified_statement: Flight altitude held within mission tolerance.

bands.label: Altitude variance >5m
bands.impact: Drone climbed or descended significantly during flight. GSD inconsistent across the dataset; reconstruction may show stretching or compression artifacts.
actions: Inspect altitude profile in flight log
Check terrain follow setting — was AGL or AMSL used correctly?
Verify no GPS altitude glitches in flight log

L3I_FC_004.derivation: 5m altitude tolerance from typical photogrammetric variance budget.

L3I_FC_005.name: Wind condition
L3I_FC_005.verified_statement: Wind conditions during flight were within drone operating spec.

bands.label: Wind ≤8 m/s — within typical drone spec
Wind 8-12 m/s — drone struggling against wind
bands.impact: High wind during flight stressed the drone's stabilization. Actual ground track may have deviated from plan; image blur or off-axis attitude possible.
actions: Inspect flight log for unusual roll/pitch values
Check images for motion blur
Postpone flights when forecast wind exceeds drone spec

bands.label: Wind >12 m/s — flight beyond drone spec
bands.impact: Wind exceeded drone operational limit. Flight integrity compromised; data quality likely degraded.
actions: Refly under calmer conditions
Check forecast vs actual wind for future trip planning
Consider drone class for high-wind operations

bands.label: Wind data unavailable (Open-Meteo API failure)
bands.impact: Could not verify wind conditions during this flight. This is an infrastructure limitation, not a flight quality concern.
actions: No action required — indicator returns null and does not affect overall score
Flight quality is judged from other indicators
If wind verification is critical, retry API lookup or check independent wind station

L3I_FC_005.derivation: Q-DRONE-4 introduces indicator-level null pattern. When Open-Meteo API unavailable, returns null + WIND_API_FALLBACK flag. Block aggregation renormalizes across measured indicators only. 8/12 m/s thresholds from typical drone manufacturer specs.

L3I_FC_006.name: Altitude consistency (in-block)
L3I_FC_006.verified_statement: Altitude held steady throughout the flight with minimal oscillation.

bands.label: Altitude oscillation >2m (hygiene signal)
bands.impact: Drone bobbed in altitude during flight. Usually wind-related. Generally doesn't affect output but creates uneven GSD.
actions: Cross-reference with wind during flight
Consider stiffer flight controller tuning if recurring
Verify GPS altitude not glitchy

L3I_FC_006.derivation: Hygiene signal, downgraded to minor — affects appearance but rarely affects measurement quality.

L3I_FC_007.name: Mission completion
L3I_FC_007.verified_statement: Mission completed cleanly without operator abort.

bands.label: Mission completed normally, no operator interrupt
Mission interrupted but completed (audit hygiene)
bands.impact: Operator paused or restarted the mission. Generally doesn't affect output, but worth knowing for incident review.
actions: Note reason for interrupt in flight log if not recorded
Verify no data gap at interrupt point
Consider operator training if interrupts are recurring

L3I_CAL_001.name: Calibration source
L3I_CAL_001.verified_statement: Camera was calibrated with a pre-existing lab calibration file.

bands.label: Lab-calibrated camera with current calibration file
Self-calibrated lens — no pre-existing calibration
bands.impact: Photogrammetry software is solving camera intrinsics from the imagery itself. Works for most cases, but adds uncertainty especially in non-flat terrain.
actions: Inspect self-calibration outputs for outlier focal length or distortion values
Consider pre-calibrating camera for high-accuracy work
Note in deliverable that self-calibration was used

bands.label: No calibration file and self-calibration also failed
bands.impact: Both pre-calibration and self-calibration unsuccessful. Photogrammetric output reliability is uncertain; expect elevated residuals.
actions: Pre-calibrate camera in lab before next survey
Inspect reconstruction residuals carefully
Consider re-flying with calibrated camera if accuracy is critical

L3I_CAL_001.derivation: Q-DRONE-5 lock: self-calibration stays material/review. Operator decides whether to insist on pre-calibration based on accuracy requirements.

L3I_CAL_002.name: Calibration match
L3I_CAL_002.verified_statement: Calibration file matches the camera that captured the imagery.

bands.label: Camera model and serial match calibration file
Camera doesn't match calibration file
bands.impact: Calibration file is for a different camera. Using it introduces systematic distortion bias. Better to self-calibrate than use wrong file.
actions: Verify which physical camera was used vs calibration file
Replace calibration file with correct one or disable
Re-process with correct calibration if available

L3I_CAL_002.derivation: Camera-calibration mismatch is more harmful than no calibration. Reviewer-blocking flag.

L3I_CAL_003.name: Calibration age
L3I_CAL_003.verified_statement: Camera calibration is recent enough to be reliable.

bands.label: Calibration 6-18 months old (audit hygiene)
bands.impact: Calibration is aging. Cameras drift over time due to handling and temperature; older calibrations are less reliable.
actions: Plan to re-calibrate in next maintenance cycle
Inspect residuals for distortion patterns
Document calibration date in deliverable metadata

bands.label: Calibration >18 months old
bands.impact: Calibration may no longer reflect current camera state. Distortion model may be outdated; output accuracy could be degraded.
actions: Re-calibrate camera in lab before next survey
Compare current and lab-calibration distortion parameters if available
Note calibration age in deliverable QA notes

L3I_CAL_003.derivation: 6-month / 18-month thresholds from photogrammetric best practice — cameras drift with handling.
